title: Add rmcp dependency and MCP subcommand skeleton
description: Add the rmcp crate as a dependency. Create a `mcp` subcommand in the CLI that starts an MCP server over stdio. The server should advertise four tools (synctell_write, synctell_read_oneshot, synctell_read_start_linger, synctell_read_stop_linger) but they don't need to do anything yet — just return placeholder responses. Wire up the subcommand dispatch in main().
acceptance_criteria: Cargo build succeeds. `synctell mcp` starts and responds to MCP initialization over stdio. The four tools appear in the tool list.

title: Implement synctell_read_stop_linger MCP tool
description: Implement the synctell_read_stop_linger MCP tool. Parameters: handle (string, required). Signals the background linger reader to stop via its AtomicBool flag, joins the thread, removes the FIFO, returns any unread messages from the buffer, and removes the entry from the global state HashMap.
acceptance_criteria: Calling synctell_read_stop_linger with a valid handle signals the background reader to stop, removes the FIFO, and cleans up. Returns any buffered messages. Calling it with an invalid handle returns an error. Tests pass.

title: Implement synctell_read_start_linger MCP tool
description: Implement the synctell_read_start_linger MCP tool. Parameters: path (string, required). Creates a FIFO and spawns a background thread that loops reading messages. Each message is queued in a thread-safe buffer (Arc<Mutex<VecDeque<Vec<u8>>>>). Returns a handle that can be used with synctell_read_stop_linger and synctell_read_poll (future tool). State management: a global HashMap of active linger readers keyed by path.
acceptance_criteria: Calling synctell_read_start_linger with a path creates a FIFO and starts a background reader. Returns a handle/ID. The FIFO stays alive accepting writers. Calling it again with the same path returns an error. Tests pass.

title: Implement synctell_read_oneshot MCP tool
description: Implement the synctell_read_oneshot MCP tool. Parameters: path (string, required), timeout (integer, optional, seconds). Creates a FIFO, blocks until a writer connects, reads one message, removes the FIFO, returns the message content. This reuses existing cmd_input/read_one_message logic but returns the data as a tool result instead of writing to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: Calling synctell_read_oneshot with a path creates the FIFO, waits for a writer, reads one message, removes the FIFO, and returns the message. Timeout returns an error with code 124. Tests pass.

title: Implement synctell_write MCP tool
description: Implement the synctell_write MCP tool. Parameters: path (string, required), message (string, required), timeout (integer, optional, seconds). The tool writes the message to the FIFO at path. If the FIFO doesn't exist and no timeout, error. If timeout, poll for up to N seconds. This reuses the existing cmd_output logic but adapted for MCP (returns structured results instead of printing to stdout).
acceptance_criteria: Calling synctell_write with a path and message creates the FIFO if needed and writes the message. Returns success confirmation. Calling it for a nonexistent reader path (without -t) returns an error with code 1. Tests pass.

title: Add integration tests for all MCP tools
description: Add integration tests for all four MCP tools. Test scenarios: 1) write → read_oneshot roundtrip, 2) write with no reader (no timeout) errors, 3) read_oneshot with timeout and no writer returns error, 4) start_linger creates FIFO and accepts writes, 5) stop_linger cleans up and returns buffered data, 6) start_linger rejects duplicate path, 7) stop_linger rejects invalid handle. Use a test helper that starts the MCP server as a child process and communicates via stdin/stdout JSON-RPC.
acceptance_criteria: Integration tests cover all 4 tools. write → oneshot read roundtrip works. write with no reader and no timeout errors properly. oneshot read with timeout and no writer errors properly. start_linger + stop_linger lifecycle works. start_linger rejects duplicate paths. All tests pass with cargo test.
